attacking_style: Dual-role rider: as sprinter, uses ultra-long 500m leadouts with late acceleration to exploit momentum differentials. In breakaways, attacks early to force selections and thin weak riders from large groups.
team_tactics: Functions as lead-out specialist for UAE with exceptional spatial awareness in finishes, identifying optimal positioning contrary to conventional wisdom (middle-right vs left on curves). Operates with minimal visible team support in breakaway scenarios.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint finishes where long sustained leadouts create momentum advantages over traditional positioning-based trains. Capable on selective stages where early aggression can shed weaker breakaway companions.
vulnerability: Timing in breakaways—showed awareness of own limitations when unable to match superior attackers like Cort. May lack punch for very short, explosive sprint finishes requiring pure positioning over momentum.
key_patterns: Deploys 500m+ leadouts, unusually long compared to standard trains, twice documented as race-winning tactic
Identifies non-conventional positioning advantages in sprint finishes, middle-right over left on curves (2 instances)
Uses early attacks in breaks to force selection and eliminate weaker riders preemptively
Demonstrates self-awareness of limitations, attacking strategically rather than following stronger finishers
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta showed elite-level leadout execution and advanced spatial intelligence, suggesting evolution into specialized sprint train asset
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 19
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 19
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 12
